shortDef.en: A compression method that reduces file size while preserving all original data exactly.

definition.en: Lossless compression is a class of data compression algorithms that allow the original data to be perfectly reconstructed from the compressed file. Not a single bit of the original information is discarded. When you decompress a losslessly compressed file, you get an exact copy of the original — byte for byte. Common lossless algorithms include DEFLATE (used in ZIP, PNG, and gzip), LZW (used in GIF and TIFF), LZMA (used in 7-Zip and XZ), and Brotli (used in web compression). Lossless compression works by finding and eliminating redundancy — repeated patterns or predictable sequences — within the data.

examples.en: PNG images use lossless DEFLATE compression — decompressing a PNG always produces the exact original pixels.
ZIP archives are losslessly compressed — extracting a ZIP gives you the original files unchanged.
FLAC audio is losslessly compressed — it sounds identical to uncompressed WAV.
